title: Pre-release codebase audit
goal: Audit the Khazad-Doom Rust codebase before v0.1.0 for AI slop coding, dirty/broken/dead/redundant code, unclear scope, and shallow modules. Keep scope limited to committed repository code and release workflow assets; either fix low-risk issues or document blockers in docs/release-audit.md.
acceptance: docs/release-audit.md exists and records audited scope, findings, fixes made, remaining risks, and release recommendation.
The audit explicitly checks module depth using the project vocabulary: module, interface, seam, depth, leverage, and locality.
Any dirty, broken, dead, redundant, or AI-slop code found inside the defined scope is either removed/fixed or listed as a justified remaining risk.
No public CLI, daemon IPC, JSON Issue Slice, or runner contract changes are made unless they are documented as required and verified.
The final worktree is clean, committed, and passes the full verification profile.
must_ask_if: The audit suggests changing public CLI behavior, daemon IPC shape, JSON worker result schema, or existing slice semantics.
A cleanup requires a broad architectural rewrite rather than a local behavior-preserving change.
A suspected issue cannot be proven with code reading or tests.
The requested scope needs to expand beyond committed Khazad-Doom repository code and release workflow assets.
